properties.titulo_evento: Pope Leo prays for Venezuela’s recovery after deadly quakes
properties.descricao: Pope Leo expressed solidarity with Venezuelans after two deadly earthquakes claimed the lives of at least 1,400.

properties.titulo_evento: Israel kills four Palestinians in Gaza as attacks on tents continue
properties.descricao: Israeli forces restrict Palestinian worshippers at Al-Aqsa Mosque and block the call to prayer at the Ibrahimi Mosque.

properties.titulo_evento: Hope of finding more Venezuela quake survivors fades despite late rescues
properties.descricao: Those rescued include 60-year-old woman trapped for 86 hours, as tens of thousands still reported missing.

properties.titulo_evento: US and Iran trade strikes days after peace agreement
properties.descricao: Just days after signing a memorandum of understanding, the US and Iran have traded strikes.

properties.titulo_evento: In Lebanon, framework agreement signed with Israel spurs protest, criticism
properties.descricao: Anger over deal that does not force Israeli troops to withdraw from occupied land after months of deadly attacks.

properties.titulo_evento: The aftermath of Venezuela’s deadly earthquake
properties.descricao: The search for survivors continues in Venezuela after a powerful earthquake left hundreds dead and thousands missing.

properties.titulo_evento: Drone captures ongoing rescue efforts after Venezuela earthquakes
properties.descricao: Drone footage from Venezuela's La Guaira shows widespread destruction after twin earthquakes devastated the region.

properties.titulo_evento: Israel strikes Lebanon, testing days-old peace deal
properties.descricao: Hezbollah calls the deal a surrender as Israeli forces stay put and continue striking the south.

properties.titulo_evento: Two boys rescued from Venezuela earthquake rubble after days of being trapped
properties.descricao: Rescuers spent six hours carefully digging to reach one of the boys, as many residents continue searching through the rubble by hand, still waiting for heavy machinery to arrive.

properties.titulo_evento: In Caracas, this feels like the hardest moment in Venezuela's modern history
properties.descricao: Rescue teams are working ceaselessly to reach those trapped under rubble. But as hope fades, anger is growing.

properties.titulo_evento: Europe's heatwave linked to 1,300 deaths, WHO says, as Germany hits record 41.7C
properties.descricao: WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warned that Europe is not prepared for high temperatures.

properties.titulo_evento: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire

properties.titulo_evento: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
properties.descricao: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

properties.titulo_evento: Bad Bunny lights up London with history-making stadium show
properties.descricao: Pride and a party as the Puerto Rican becomes the first Latin artist to headline a UK stadium.

properties.titulo_evento: Watch: Inside the Venezuela country club now a makeshift hospital
properties.descricao: Survivors of Venezuela's devastating earthquakes are being cared for at a makeshift hospital formed on the site of a country club in Caraballeda.

properties.titulo_evento: Watch: A race against the clock for Venezuela earthquake rescuers
properties.descricao: There is a sense that the next few hours will be critical for saving anyone who is still trapped, says the BBC's Dan Johnson reporting from the border of Venezuela and Columbia.

properties.titulo_evento: Newborn baby rescued from Venezuela earthquake rubble

properties.titulo_evento: Israel formally recognises Armenian World War I 'genocide'
properties.descricao: Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Sa'ar said Israel had "fulfilled a moral duty by recognizing the historical truth, and rejecting attempts to deny it".

properties.titulo_evento: More than 68,000 people still missing in Venezuela after devastating earthquakes

properties.descricao: More than 520 emergency responders from across the EU have been mobilised to support search and rescue efforts in Venezuela, the European Commission said.

properties.descricao: As US restrictions on oil shipments have left Cuba struggling to secure fuel, China is helping the communist island accelerate one of the world's fastest solar transitions. Imports of solar panels and batteries have surged over the past year, allowi...

properties.titulo_evento: Europe swelters under record heat
properties.descricao: Tens of millions of people are enduring extreme temperatures across Europe as a deadly heatwave pushes eastward. Scientists say a heatwave of this intensity so early in the summer would have been virtually impossible without human-caused climate cha...

properties.titulo_evento: Russia's Putin promises security as Ukraine ramps up attacks
properties.descricao: The Russian leader condemned what he called "terrorist attacks on our territory and infrastructure facilities." Ukraine has targeted oil refineries and weapons factories, causing massive disruption in recent weeks.

properties.titulo_evento: Argentina: Milei's top aide resigns over corruption scandal
properties.descricao: Manuel Adorni is mired in a corruption scandal but has denied wrongdoing. Javier Milei has defended Adorni, saying, "Manuel is innocent."

properties.titulo_evento: Venezuela earthquakes: Nearly 7 million may be impacted — UN
properties.descricao: The projection includes up to two million people in the capital, Caracas. Rescue teams are racing against time to save survivors, three days after the twin quakes that killed 1,450 people. DW has more.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine strikes Russian weapons plant, Moscow fuel hub
properties.descricao: The two attacks, in Volgograd and Vladimir regions, are part of President Zelenskyy's strategy to bring the war to the Russian people and force Putin into peace negotiations.

properties.titulo_evento: Why is the US targeting Germany's drug industry?
properties.descricao: A new trade probe is reigniting a long-running clash over who should pay for medical innovation. Washington argues Germans benefit from lower prices while US patients shoulder a disproportionate share of the costs.

properties.titulo_evento: Rome celebrates 80 years of Italy's iconic Vespa scooter
properties.descricao: Thousands of Vespa riders took to Rome's streets to mark Vespa's 80th birthday. The wasp-waisted scooter became a symbol of Italy's rebirth after World War II.

properties.titulo_evento: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
properties.descricao: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

properties.titulo_evento: Why Germany's Deutsche Bahn will face delays for many years
properties.descricao: Delayed, unreliable and forced into long detours due to construction: Germany no longer sets the pace when it comes to rail travel — and this is unlikely to change anytime soon. The company is mired in a deep crisis.

properties.titulo_evento: Military-age Ukrainian men could lose EU protection
properties.descricao: The European Commission has suggested withdrawing protection status for military-age Ukrainian males. A number of EU countries support the move — and so does Kyiv.

properties.titulo_evento: Vatican accuses EU of double standards on war
properties.descricao: Doctrinal chief in Rome says the European Union applies international law selectively, sanctioning some military invasions but not others.

properties.titulo_evento: In Strait of Hormuz, Renewed Strikes Threaten to Set Back Shipping Recovery
properties.descricao: The attacks came after traffic through the Strait of Hormuz had reached the highest levels since the start of the U.S. war in Iran.
